chunk_summary: This section explores digital and teleassessment tools for dietary evaluation, emphasizing their role in health system strengthening, anemia management, and malnutrition tracking.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of digital tools in dietary assessment and feeding skills evaluation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of teleassessment methods in addressing malnutrition and anemia.
Analyze the use of digital food frequency questionnaires validated against 24-hour recalls.
Explore global and regional progress in leveraging data for nutrition program improvements.
key_facts: Digital interventions are recommended by WHO for health system strengthening (WHO, 2019).
A digital food frequency questionnaire was validated using 24-hour recalls as a reference method (PMC10882527).
Anemia-focused digital interventions have been analyzed for their landscape and impact (PATH report).
India's progress in addressing malnutrition is tracked using data-driven approaches (DataDENT POSHA report).
Teleassessment tools can enhance dietary evaluation in remote or underserved areas.
reference_quote: Recommendations on digital interventions for health system strengthening (WHO, 2019).

subtopic: DIGITAL AND TELEASSESSMENT ENABLED DIETARY EVALUATION
